title: quercus andegavensis subsp. subandegavensis
intro: Quercus andegavensis subsp. subandegavensis is a subspecies of the Andegavensis oak tree, native to the mountainous regions of western Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its rich content of tannins, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Quercus andegavensis subsp. subandegavensis include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
